title: geonoma lehmannii subsp. lehmannii
intro: Geonoma lehmannii subsp. lehmannii, also known as the Lehmann's geonoma or Puya de Lehmann, is a species of palm tree native to the Andean region. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Geonoma lehmannii subsp. lehmannii has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and rheumatism, thanks to its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
